creator: anders@fix.no
text:
I updated the ntop port to version 1.2a1. This version supports neped
(Network Promiscuous Ethernet Detector), queso and lsof as well, for
various purposes. I disabled the queso support as it seems to freeze ntop
while in web mode, and neped as it seems to use Linux specific ways of
getting packets at device level etc.

Anyway, consider these patches instead:

diff -Nur ntop.old/files/md5 ntop/files/md5 
--- ntop.old/files/md5  Fri May  7 16:32:29 1999
+++ ntop/files/md5      Thu May 13 05:05:09 1999
@@ -1 +1 @@
-MD5 (ntop-1.1-src.tar.gz) = 4630ed141d9d5f16f929a9b64b70a45c
+MD5 (ntop-1.2a1.tar.gz) = ee6e5981277c0a7dee84d2e742ef6144

diff -Nur ntop.old/Makefile ntop/Makefile 
--- ntop.old/Makefile   Sun May  9 19:41:41 1999
+++ ntop/Makefile       Tue May 18 18:13:51 1999
@@ -6,10 +6,9 @@
 # $Id: Makefile,v 1.9 1999/05/07 19:31:59 billf Exp $
 #
 
-DISTNAME=      ntop-1.1-src
-PKGNAME=       ntop-1.1
+DISTNAME=      ntop-1.2a1
 CATEGORIES=    net
-MASTER_SITES=  ftp://ftp.unipi.it/pub/local/ntop/source/ \
+MASTER_SITES=  ftp://ftp.unipi.it/pub/local/ntop/snapshots/ \
                http://www.freenix.no/~anders/ \
                ftp://totem.fix.no/pub/mirrors/misc/
 
@@ -18,11 +17,21 @@
 HAS_CONFIGURE= yes
 ALL_TARGET=
 INSTALL_TARGET=        install install-man
-WRKSRC=                ${WRKDIR}/${PKGNAME}
+CONFIGURE_ARGS=        --disable-neped \
+               --enable-lsof \
+               --disable-queso
+# Change --enable-lsof to --disable-lsof if you do not want do monitor
+# local network usage by process/port.
+CONFIGURE_ENV= QUESO_PATH=${PREFIX}/sbin/queso \
+               LSOF_PATH=${PREFIX}/sbin/lsof
+
+RUN_DEPENDS=   lsof:${PORTSDIR}/sysutils/lsof
 
 MAN8=          ntop.8
 
 post-install:
        strip ${PREFIX}/sbin/ntop
+       ${INSTALL} -d -m 0755 ${PREFIX}/share/ntop/html
+       ${INSTALL} -c -m 0744 ${WRKSRC}/html/* ${PREFIX}/share/ntop/html
 
 .include <bsd.port.mk>

-- 
Anders Nordby ^ anders@fix.no ^ http://anders.fix.no/
